name: Stuffed Chicken, Cyprus Style
recipeIngredient: 3 tbsp Vegetable oil or butter             1 ts Ground cinnamon
1/2 cup Blanched, slivered almonds          1 pn Granulated sugar
3/4 cup Raw long-grain white rice         1/2 c  Currants
1/2 cup Dry white wine                           Melted butter or veg. oil
recipeInstructions:
1 Wash and dry the chicken, reserving the liver, and set aside.
2 In a medium saucepan, heat the oil or butter and sauté the almonds and liver. Remove with a slotted spoon.
3 Chop the liver and set aside with the nuts.
4 Add the rice to the fat in the pan and sauté over medium heat, stirring constantly.
5 Pour in the wine, water, salt, cinnamon, and sugar. Cover and cook for 12 minutes, or until the rice is almost tender.
6 Stir in the almonds, liver, and currants. Remove the pan from the heat.
7 Spoon the stuffing into the large cavity of the chicken and close tightly with skewers.
8 Truss the chicken and brush the surface lightly with melted butter or oil.
9 Set in a baking pan, breast side up, and bake for 1 1/4 hours, or until tender (350 F). Turn with two wooden spoons every 20 minutes and baste frequently with drippings.
10 Remove the stuffing from the cavity and transfer to the center of a warm platter.
11 Carve the chicken and arrange around the stuffing. Serve warm.
Note: For a popular mainland version, substitute pine nuts, nutmeg, and a little chopped celery and parsley, and soaked bread for the almonds, cinnamon, and rice.
